title: Echoes in the Empty Lounge
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitaker, senior museum curator, was found dead in a cluttered lounge with a shoe, remote control, water bottle and backpack. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Harlow
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the water bottle and did not go near the shoe.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Miriam Calder
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Trevor Finch helped me move the remote control aside. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Gareth Harlow and Eleanor Whitaker were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Trevor Finch and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Trevor Finch
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Miriam Calder move the remote control aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Miriam Calder and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

clues.title: Granules in Water Bottle
clues.detail: Undissolved blue granules cling below the liquid line inside the water bottle. The same blue residue is visible at Eleanor Whitaker's lips and in the remaining drink.

clues.title: Position of Remote Control
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the remote control is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink whose edge remains unbroken. Miriam Calder and Trevor Finch both state that they moved the remote control aside at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Water Bottle
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle and rim of the water bottle. Gareth Harlow's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Shoe
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged in a narrow edge of the shoe on top of a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Gareth Harlow has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Mark on Backpack
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the backpack. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Gareth Harlow killed Eleanor Whitaker because she had uncovered his illegal sale of priceless artefacts to a private collector.
solution.method: A fatal crushed dose was dissolved in the water bottle before Eleanor Whitaker drank.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Gareth Harlow claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Miriam Calder and Trevor Finch left together at 8:35 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the water bottle and the black and gold fragment recovered from the shoe place Gareth Harlow inside afterwards. Gareth Harlow therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitaker and reach the water bottle during the murder window.
